questions.prompt: The terminal displays a security code list [2,7,11,15]. The system unlocks only if you find the sum of the two positions whose values add up to the target 9. Enter the index sum.
A damaged access log shows the sequence [0,1,2,4,5]. One ID is missing from the sequence. Recover the missing value.
The trading floor scanner recorded IDs [4,1,9,6,1]. One ID was accidentally duplicated. Identify the repeated ID.
The market terminal displays stock prices [7,1,5,3,6] over several days. Find the maximum profit possible by choosing the best time to buy and sell once.
A courier robot must cross a 3 x 7 city grid to deliver a package. It can only travel right or down. Calculate the number of unique routes from the start point to the destination.
A vault door uses the bracket pattern (()()) as its authentication sequence. Enter 1 if the pattern is valid; otherwise enter the index of the first invalid symbol.
The archive terminal stores sorted access keys [3,9,14,22,31,40]. Locate the key 22 and enter its 0-based position.
A server records network load changes [-2,4,-1,3,-2,2]. Find the largest continuous period of positive gain. Enter the maximum total change.
A machine analyzes the array [2,3,4]. Replace the value at index 1 with the product of all other values. Enter the new value.

locations.notes: Camera open-palm verification gates the door into the Bay Control Center room after Stage 3's hint (7) unlocks; inside, the player enters 577 (fallback: type it directly if the terminal template is used instead of a keypad).

questions.prompt: The trading vault requires a dice verification code. Two dice are rolled, creating 36 possible outcomes. Count how many outcomes produce a total of exactly 7.
A security analyst checks a standard 52-card deck. The odds of drawing an Ace are written as "1 in ___". Determine the missing number.
Six traders meet before the market opens. Each trader shakes hands with every other trader exactly once. Calculate the total number of handshakes.
A random security system flips a fair coin repeatedly until the first HEAD appears. Determine the expected number of flips needed to unlock the system.

A market analyst inspects a bin containing 5 blue tokens and 3 red tokens. Two tokens are drawn without replacement. Find the numerator of the probability that both selected tokens are red (the denominator is 28).

correction.note: P(both red) = (3/8)(2/7) = 6/56 = 3/28. Over the stated denominator 28 the numerator is 3, not 6 (6 is the numerator over 56).

questions.prompt: A game host presents three vault doors, with only one containing the prize. After you choose a door, the host opens a losing door. Decide whether the optimal strategy is to SWITCH doors or STAY with your original choice.

The final security chamber contains 20 locked doors. For each round k = 1 through 20, the system toggles every door whose number is a multiple of k. After all rounds finish, determine how many doors remain open.

A vault contains two normal coins and one double-headed coin. A random coin is selected and flipped once, resulting in HEADS. Calculate the percentage chance that the chosen coin was the double-headed coin.
A trader evaluates a new strategy with possible returns: +$12 with 25% probability, +$2 with 50% probability, and -$4 with 25% probability. Calculate the expected value of the trade.

locations.notes: Correction (was 497): recomputed Stage 2 numerator/hint per ny_balls_no_replace correction, giving final NYC code 467.
